openingMessage: Nobody wins alone. Nobody builds a company alone. Nobody protects a location alone. We succeed by winning the small daily battles.
leadershipThought: Assume people are trying to do the right thing. But standards do not move. You can hold both.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-23):
• Larry Taylor: SM Energy Gipple 134 — bleeding well off to flare stack, rig shut down for maintenance, waiting on mechanic (4:47 AM).
• John Delagarza: AOL at Maralo HL Fed Com #1H (5:44 AM).
• Freddy V: H&P 264 — 5,661" hole depth, alarms tested, all equipment confirmed (5:47 AM).
• Ralph Forbes: On Pratt Vaughn 151 #109h (6:02 AM) → On Site University 6-48a #101H (10:28 AM). Waiting on Exxon update for job status (1:40 PM).
• Jacob Hernandez: Arrived on Poppy Atrelle (early AM) → Arrived on Williams — nail rig still on well, rigging down, waiting on MT (11:05 AM) → Job started (1:44 PM).
• Nathan Contreras: Job done, heading to next location (10:23 AM) → Arrived on Mary 18-7-H 4415H (10:48 AM).
• Alex Clubb: Christi — vac truck pulling bulk separator, strong smell with monitors going off at 10:44 AM. Gas coming from unidentified line at end of vac truck — not the vent line. 31 ppm H2S on crew, mustered upwind. Update 1:39 PM: 97 ppm H2S off bleeder on gas line from bulk separator. Update 2:53 PM: going back under air to close manway, returning Wednesday to tackle sludge and gases from vessel.
• Michael Hutt: SM Coonskin pad — job extended through June 29 (1:39 PM). Gated out end of shift (4:35 PM).
• Dawson Drake: Both crews done — cleaned out a new separator and finished the one from yesterday (2:56 PM).
• Marcos Morales: Job at Glass L #3 cancelled and rescheduled, heading back to shop (3:07 PM).
• David Hammond: "In each binder there are these laminated" — shared Primary Emergency Hospitals card being added to crew binders (4:33 PM).

Carry-Forward:
• [ ] **Alex Clubb — Christi**: Back Wednesday to tackle sludge and gases from vessel. Air monitoring and supplied air required from arrival.
• [ ] **Michael Hutt — SM Coonskin**: Extended through June 29. Confirm monitoring plan and H2S status before crew visits.
• [ ] **Ralph Forbes — University 6-48A #101H**: Confirm Exxon job status update before mobilizing.
• [ ] **Dennis Easley / Larry Taylor — Gipple**: SM Energy Gipple 134 rig down for maintenance (06-23). Gipple 124H H2S run (Dennis — ongoing). Confirm both locations have current escalation and monitoring plan.

fieldNews.headline: Dawson Drake
fieldNews.detail: Two crews done in one day: cleaned a new separator AND finished the one from the day before. Clean two-day execution on a heavy job.

fieldNews.headline: Freddy V (H&P 264)
fieldNews.detail: 5:47 AM shift check-in with hole depth, alarms tested, and all equipment confirmed. Solid rig discipline right at shift start.

fieldNews.headline: Alex Clubb
fieldNews.detail: Transparent, real-time communication on a developing H2S situation across three separate updates over 4+ hours. Mustered immediately at 31 ppm. Set proper expectations for Wednesday return to vessel.

fieldNews.headline: David Hammond
fieldNews.detail: Proactively sharing laminated emergency hospital cards being added to crew binders across the fleet. Good resource awareness initiative.

todaysChallenge: Before you speak on the next job — ask yourself: What outcome am I trying to create?
todaysChallengeSubtext: If you can't answer that, don't speak yet. Outcome-driven communication solves problems. Emotion-driven communication creates them.
